sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a little strain and mild lateral neck pain. He denies any blood thinners. He reports intermittent left arm pain, and he had a pinched nerve according to a previous evaluation. He is currently taking eliquis, gabapentin, and a neck pill.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient is an elderly white gentleman presenting in a wheelchair. Cervical x-ray reveals significant disc degeneration at C5-C6, and to a lower extent C4-C5 and C3-C4. Gait was not assessed today. Physical exam reveals restricted cervical extension.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient is suspected to have cervical radicularopathy, likely exacerbated by disc degeneration. He has a history of peripheral neuropathy.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I am ordering a cervical MRI to determine the exact location of nerve compression. I am also setting up a left C7-T1 epidural. The patient will continue with his home exercise program and twice-daily gabapentin. I will order a cervical x-ray 6421.
